meaning: n.(美、法) 百万的三次方,(英、德)百万的五次方
examples: The Planck energy is a quintillion times higher than the LHC's maximum.
"Exascale" means it will be capable of making a quintillion (1 followed by 18 zeros) calculations per second.
Intel estimates that about 10 quintillion (or a 1 followed by 19 zeros) transistors ship each year. That 10,000 times the number of ants on Earth.
An exabyte is a unit of information or computer storage equal to 1 quintillion bytes.
Every day all this new digital activity is creating 2.5 quintillion bytes of data.
